# terrible_cisco_aci_aci_maintenance_policy (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against

### Optional

- `admin_state` (String) The administrative state of the executable policies. Will trigger an immediate upgrade for nodes if `admin_state` is set to triggered. The APIC defaults to `untriggered` when unset during creation.
- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `description` (String) Description for the maintenance policy.
- `download_state` (String) The download state of the executable policies. The APIC defaults to `untriggered` when unset during creation.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `graceful` (Boolean) Whether the system will bring down the nodes gracefully during an upgrade, which reduces traffic lost. The APIC defaults to `false` when unset during creation.
- `ignore_compat` (Boolean) To check whether compatibility checks should be ignored The APIC defaults to `false` when unset during creation.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `name` (String) The name for the maintenance policy.
- `name_alias` (String) The alias for the current object. This relates to the nameAlias field in ACI.
- `notify_condition` (String) Specifies under what pause condition will admin be notified via email/text as configured. This notification mechanism is independent of events/faults. The APIC defaults to `notifyOnlyOnFailures` when unset during creation.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `run_mode` (String) Whether the system pauses on error or just continues through it. The APIC defaults to `pauseOnlyOnFailures` when unset during creation.
- `scheduler` (String) The name of scheduler that is applied to the policy.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `smu_operation` (String) Specifies that the upgrade is a Software Maintenance Upgrade (SMU) patch operation.
- `smu_operation_flags` (String) Specifies the Software Maintenance Upgrade (SMU) patch operation flags Indicates if node should be reloaded immediately or skip auto reload on SMU Install/Uninstall.
- `sr_upgrade` (Boolean) Specifies that the upgrade is a Silent Roll (SR) package upgrade.
- `sr_version` (String) The target firmware version of the Silent Roll (SR) package upgrade to install.
- `state` (String) Use `present` or `absent` for adding or removing. Use `query` for listing an object or multiple objects.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `version` (String) The target firmware version to install.
- `version_check_override` (String) The version check override. This is a directive to ignore the version check for the next install. The version check, which occurs during a maintenance window, checks to see if the desired version matches the running version. If the versions do not match, the install is performed. If the versions do match, the install is not performed. The version check override is a one-time override that performs the install whether or not the versions match. The APIC defaults to `untriggered` when unset during creation.

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `current` (String) The existing configuration from the APIC after the module has finished
- `error` (String) The error information as returned from the APIC
- `filter_string` (String) The filter string used for the request
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
- `method` (String) The HTTP method used for the request to the APIC
- `previous` (String) The original configuration from the APIC before the module has started
- `proposed` (String) The assembled configuration from the user-provided parameters
- `raw` (String) The raw output returned by the APIC REST API (xml or json)
- `response` (String) The HTTP response from the APIC
- `sent` (String) The actual/minimal configuration pushed to the APIC
- `status` (Number) The HTTP status from the APIC
- `url` (String) The HTTP url used for the request to the APIC
